author: Dr. Lizard
title: Why are factor B inhibitors theoretically superior to C3 inhibitors for PNH?
answer: Factor B inhibitors block C3 convertase formation while preserving partial C3 function, reducing infection risk and breakthrough hemolysis.
detail: Factor B is essential for forming C3bBb, the alternative pathway C3 convertase that drives PNH pathology.
Blocking factor B prevents both intravascular and extravascular hemolysis without completely eliminating C3 opsonization of pathogens.
Iptacopan is an oral factor B inhibitor, offering convenient twice-daily dosing versus subcutaneous pegcetacoplan injections.
Factor B inhibition avoids the enzymatic amplification risk seen with incomplete C3 blockade during breakthrough events.
releaseNote: Factor B inhibitors are theoretically superior to C3 inhibitors for PNH due to their ability to block C3 convertase formation while preserving partial C3 function, reducing infection risk and breakthrough hemolysis.
